subject: Physics
title: Millikan's Oil Drop Experiment
description: A short lesson to help you apply Millikan's Oil Drop Experiment.

hook.text: Robert Millikan measured the charge of a single electron by watching tiny oil drops hover between electric plates.

buildup.text: He sprayed oil drops into a chamber and ionized them with X-rays, giving them a few electron charges.

discovery.text: By adjusting the electric field, he balanced gravity on each drop. From the balance, he calculated the charge.

twist.text: All charges were exact multiples of the same value: 1.6 × 10^-19 coulombs. Charge comes in discrete packets.

climax.text: Millikan won the 1923 Nobel Prize. His measured value is still used in every physics textbook.

punchline.text: Floating oil drops measured nature's smallest charge. Elegant and precise.

quiz.question: What did Millikan's oil drop experiment measure?
options: The electric charge of a single electron
The mass of an oil molecule
The speed of light through oil
